capability_objective: Resolve Winter shelter checklist in Beacon Program and Spring festival runbook in Clover Program with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Spring festival runbook is still blocked, change only Spring festival runbook from blocked to ready. Leave Winter shelter checklist unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify project coordinator with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. Report only returned fields, and label any missing requested detail as unknown. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two project records in the supplied sandbox through the genuine local `./projectctl` executable, using Pi's Bash tool. Begin by reading the executable's top-level help. Treat the executable as the only interface to project records and notices: do not inspect or edit it, its backing files, its operation journal, its notification store, or anything under `.protected`. Do not manufacture operation evidence or create a helper script, function, loop, wrapper, or scratch file for the workflow.

Resolve the exact record named `Winter shelter checklist` in `Beacon Program` and the exact record named `Spring festival runbook` in `Clover Program`. Start the two independent scoped searches as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one project operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, use shell backgrounding, broaden either search, or start a retrieval until both calls have completed. Continue only if each search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If either lookup is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval, mutation, notification, and report; identify the unresolved lookup and do not guess.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete records by their respective returned IDs. Start the two independent retrievals as two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes `projectctl`, again with exactly one project operation in each call. Use only the IDs returned by the corresponding searches and wait for both retrievals. If either retrieval fails, does not match its search result, or lacks a status, stop before mutation, notification, and report. Base the condition only on the complete retrieved Spring festival runbook record, never on a search summary.

If and only if the retrieved Spring festival runbook status is exactly `blocked`, conditionally change only its returned stable ID from `blocked` to `ready`. Leave Winter shelter checklist and every same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and out-of-program record unchanged. If the condition does not hold, skip both the mutation and notification. If the mutation fails or its response does not confirm a successful change to `ready`, stop and do not notify. Only after a successful mutation, send exactly one later notice to `project coordinator` using the exact nonempty outcome returned by that mutation. Do not notify under any other condition. Do not list, create, cancel, issue another update or notice, or directly write project state or operation evidence.

After the permitted project path succeeds, use Pi's Write tool to create `project-outcome.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly six newline-terminated, nonblank lines with no heading or extra prose, in this structure:
`| Name | ID | Program | Owner | Due date | Retrieved status |`
`| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |`
`| <first returned name> | <first returned ID> | <first returned program> | <first returned owner> | <first returned due date> | <first returned status> |`
`| <second returned name> | <second returned ID> | <second returned program> | <second returned owner> | <second returned due date> | <second returned status> |`
`Update: <returned ID> | <returned before status> -> <returned status> | <returned outcome>`
`Notification: <returned recipient> | <returned delivered value> | <returned outcome>`
Use only fields returned by the successful full-record, update, and notification operations, with Winter shelter checklist on the first data line and Spring festival runbook on the second. Preserve returned strings exactly. Render every absent or null requested field as the lowercase word `unknown`, and render a returned JSON boolean as lowercase `true` or `false`. Do not include notes, unrelated records, inferred details, or any other fields. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the report, leave supplied and protected files unchanged, and make the final assistant response exactly the report's content.
